description: PUH-BrianMartell-T212 does in plain language: •  The weak force (W⁺, W⁻, Z bosons + left-handed doublets + parity violation) is not added by hand — it sits exactly inside E8. •  E8 breaks in two symmetric ways: one gives the strong force (E8 → E6 × SU(3), T211), the other gives the weak force (E8 → E7 × SU(2), this paper). •  The 248 generators of E8 split as: 248 = 133 (E7 stuff) + 3 (weak gauge bosons) + 112 (matter doublets) → The 3 come from the SU(2) adjoint → W¹, W², W³ (which become W± and Z after breaking). → The 112 come from E7’s 56-plet × SU(2) doublet → exactly the left-handed weak doublets of the Standard Model. •  When you further break SU(2) → U(1) (the T₃ generator), the doublets become the (56, ±1) fields that were flagged as Open Problem F. So OP-F is now structurally closed.
